word_list.word: the caribou feeds the wolf but it is the wolf that keeps the caribou strong

senses.definition: used to suggest that, just as the caribou is kept strong by the presence of the wolf, individuals can be strengthened by overcoming obstacles or difficulties

word_list.word: dogs of the same street bark alike

senses.definition: used to imply that people who share common backgrounds, experiences, or interests tend to have similar opinions, behaviors, or characteristics

word_list.word: the hand that rocks the cradle rules the world

senses.definition: used to suggest that caregivers have a significant influence on the development, attitudes, and behaviors of children, and that this influence can shape the future of society

examples.x: The influential teacher who inspires and shapes the minds of young students understands the power of their role, for the hand that rocks the cradle rules the world, as they have the potential to shape future leaders and change the course of society.

word_list.word: laugh and the (whole|) world laughs with you (; weep and you weep alone|)
word_list.pronunciation: lňą√¶f √¶nd √į…ô hňąo äl wňą…úňźld lňą√¶fz w…™√į juňź wňąiňźp √¶nd juňź wňąiňźp …źlňąo än

senses.definition: used to emphasize that through their laughter, individuals can influence the mood and reactions of those around them

examples.x: She was feeling down, but I reminded her that laugh and the world laughs with you.

senses.definition: used to suggest that people tend to adopt similar beliefs, characteristics, and behaviors to those with whom they spend the most time

examples.x: He saw that his unhealthy habits were influencing his children's behavior, as like breeds like.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the behavior and attitudes of a leader or authority figure are reflected in their subordinates or followers
used to imply that the attitudes and behaviors of a group of people are reflected in those who represent them, and that individuals tend to choose leaders who share their values and beliefs
used to emphasize that negative behavior or attitudes can be contagious and spread from one person to another, creating a cycle of negative behavior
used to suggest that words can be a powerful tool for influencing people's thoughts and actions, and can be more effective than physical force in bringing about change

examples.x: She wanted to make a difference in the world, so she decided to become a journalist, knowing that the pen is mightier than sword.

word_list.word: money makes the world go round
word_list.pronunciation: mňą Ćni mňĆe…™ks √į…ô wňą…úňźld …°ňĆo ä …Ļňąa änd

senses.definition: used to suggest that money is the driving force behind many of the world's activities and events, and that financial gain is often prioritized over other important values

examples.x: In the modern world, it often feels like money makes the world go round, with financial resources becoming increasingly important in determining political power and social status.

senses.definition: used to suggest that personal feelings and relationships can play a significant role in how people choose to show their affection, support, or favor

examples.x: The politician was known to give preferential treatment to his supporters, showing that kissing goes by favor in the political arena.

senses.definition: used to imply that even small or seemingly insignificant things can have a significant impact or cause great harm when they accumulate or are left unchecked

word_list.word: praise makes good men better and bad men worse
word_list.pronunciation: p…Ļňąe…™z mňĆe…™ks …°ňą äd mňą…õn bňą…õ…ĺ…ö √¶nd bňą√¶d mňą…õn wňą…úňźs

senses.definition: used to suggest that praise can encourage virtuous behavior in good people and reinforce negative behavior in bad people

examples.x: I've always believed that praise makes good men better and bad men worse, because it can either motivate or inflate their egos.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the behavior and attitudes of a leader or authority figure are reflected in their subordinates or followers
